what_it_does: Defines a React Workshop canvas for creating, opening, editing, transforming, templating, and exporting document-style deliverables, including deck, brochure, widget, wizard, registry-backed doc types, block/page editing, and Vi-assisted generation flows.
observations: The file uses globals on window for registry access, AI actions, block/layout/theme definitions, builder components, export UI, toasts, icons, and variable systems.
It has list and edit modes; widget and wizard docs route to dedicated builders, while deck and brochure docs use the Workshop editing surface.
Document mutation is handled through saveDoc callbacks with immutable page and section updates.
AI flows cover draft generation, candidate galleries, field alternates, whole-doc transforms, block refinement, section variations, page filling, layout proposals, and template variable extraction.
The custom doc type picker subscribes to CV_REGISTRY and displays user or Vi authored doc Types separately from built-in DOC_TYPES.
standouts: A shared candidate gallery is reused for field regeneration, block insertion, alternates, layout proposals, and document transforms.
The Workshop component acts as an orchestration surface over many window-provided subsystems.
The edit model is page and section data mutation rather than route-level document editing.
conventions: React hooks are aliased with Workshop-specific suffixes such as useState_w and useEffect_w.
Most Workshop behaviors are implemented as nested functions inside the Workshop component.
IDs are generated with Date.now() and sometimes an index suffix.
Updates copy docs, pages, and sections with object spreads, map, filter, and splice on copied arrays.
Prompts request compact JSON and route responses through parseJsonLoose.
concerns: Many required collaborators are read from window without local guards before use.
Timestamp-based IDs can collide during rapid repeated operations.
Undo and redo depend on serialized snapshots and saveDoc timing, which makes history behavior sensitive to persistence semantics.
Several AI catch blocks collapse detailed errors into generic toasts.
The custom registry picker passes a second argument to onPick, while newDoc only accepts type in the shown code.
Page and section locked flags are toggled, but the shown mutator functions do not consistently enforce those locks.
showSuggestions and WSErrorBoundary are initialized in the shown Workshop scope without visible use.
notes: This file is the Workshop authoring surface that wires document state, block editing, Vi generation, template extraction, and registry doc type discovery into one canvas.
questions: Should locked pages and locked sections block local editing operations, AI operations, or both?
How should registry-authored doc Type metadata affect new document creation beyond choosing a fallback family?
Which window globals are guaranteed to exist before this canvas renders?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the visual canvas role; the actual file also includes active widget and wizard flows plus registry-backed custom doc type handling beyond the header note.
intention_signals: It seems aimed at making Workshop an authoring cockpit where users create deliverables from typed pages, blocks, layouts, registry Types, and Vi-generated alternatives while keeping save, export, and template reuse in one flow.
novelty: The file combines a reusable AI candidate gallery, registry-backed document type discovery, page/block editing, and template-variable extraction in a single authoring canvas.
contribution: Contributes a Workshop orchestration layer for document creation and editing flows, page and section mutation operations, candidate-selection AI workflows, template extraction preview, registry-backed custom document type picking, and deck/brochure editing rails.
summary_for_embedding: React Workshop canvas for creating and editing structured deliverables with deck, brochure, widget, wizard, registry doc type, AI candidate, template, export, page, and section workflows.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for turning ConceptV deliverables into an interactive authoring workspace where manual composition and Vi-assisted generation share the same document model.

symbols.does: Returns registry doc Types authored by user or Vi for the doc picker.
symbols.description: It subscribes to CV_REGISTRY changes, queries layer doc, and filters to provenance user or vi. It keeps built-in DOC_TYPES out of the custom registry group.

symbols.does: Renders the Workshop canvas and owns document list, draft, edit, AI, history, export, and template flows.
symbols.description: It switches between list and edit views, routes widget and wizard docs to dedicated builders, and renders deck or brochure editing with library, preview, outline, variables, suggestions, candidate gallery, and template modal. It receives persistence callbacks and mutates docs through saveDoc.

symbols.does: Closes the whole-doc transform menu on document clicks.
symbols.description: It is registered only while the transform menu is open and removed by effect cleanup. The menu trigger wrapper stops propagation so menu-local clicks do not immediately close it.

symbols.does: Starts save-as-template by asking WS_AI to extract variables from a doc.
symbols.description: It sets an extracting preview state, stores extracted variables and doc data for modal confirmation, and falls back to saving a plain cloned template on failure. It reports extraction failure through dsaToast.

symbols.does: Persists the template preview as a workshop-doc template.
symbols.description: It guards against missing or still-extracting preview state, calls saveTemplate with generated id, name, source doc type, extracted doc, variables, and saved date, then closes the preview and emits a toast.

symbols.does: Deep-clones JSON-compatible data by serializing and parsing it.
symbols.description: It is used as a fallback template copy helper. Its behavior is limited to JSON-safe values.

symbols.does: Opens the candidate gallery and streams generated candidates into it.
symbols.description: It seeds gallery state with a unique id, calls WS_AI.generateCandidatesStream with doc, target, count, and brief, appends each candidate, and clears busy state on completion or failure. It supports an override callback for field-level candidate application.

symbols.does: Applies the selected gallery candidate and closes the gallery.
symbols.description: If an override is present, it delegates application to that callback; otherwise it applies cand.diff through WS_AI.applyDiff after computing an inverse diff. It emits an applied toast.

symbols.does: Regenerates or extends candidates for the open gallery using a hint.
symbols.description: For more options it appends three distinct additional candidates; otherwise it clears the gallery and regenerates three candidates using the hint as a brief. It keeps state scoped by the gallery id.

symbols.does: Routes a transform menu choice into a candidate gallery run.
symbols.description: It closes the transform menu and either uses the transform's direct target or wraps its instruction in a doc.transform target. The gallery title names the chosen transform.

symbols.does: Handles Workshop edit-mode keyboard shortcuts.
symbols.description: It ignores input and contenteditable focus, then handles undo, redo, deck page duplication, deck left/right page navigation, and Escape selection clearing. It is attached only while the edit view is active.

symbols.does: Initializes draft state for a selected document type.
symbols.description: It stores the requested type, clears the brief, and sets widget or wizard kind and system defaults to auto where applicable. Other doc types clear kind and system state.

symbols.does: Creates a new document from a blank template or AI draft.
symbols.description: It generates an id, optionally calls type-specific Vi draft functions, falls back to blank pages and extras on failure, saves the new doc with default editorial theme, opens it for editing, and resets selection, variations, and history.

symbols.does: Opens an existing doc for editing.
symbols.description: It stores the doc id, switches to edit view, resets current page and selection, clears variation state, and empties undo and redo history.

symbols.does: Returns from edit view to the document list.
symbols.description: It clears docId and selectedIdx while setting view to list.

symbols.does: Applies a doc replacement or updater function through saveDoc.
symbols.description: It no-ops without an active doc. When passed a function, it calls that function with the current doc before saving.

symbols.does: Restores the previous serialized doc snapshot.
symbols.description: It requires at least two undo snapshots, moves the current snapshot to redo, saves the previous snapshot while suppressing the next history capture, and reports the action with a toast.

symbols.does: Restores the next serialized doc snapshot from redo history.
symbols.description: It pops a redo snapshot, pushes it back to undo history, saves it while suppressing the next history capture, and reports the action with a toast.

symbols.does: Replaces one section on one page.
symbols.description: It maps pages and sections immutably, swapping the targeted section with the provided newSec.

symbols.does: Deletes one section from one page.
symbols.description: It filters the target page's sections by index and clears the selected section state afterward.

symbols.does: Adds a new block section at a page index.
symbols.description: It looks up block defaults from window.WS_BLOCKS, creates a timestamp-based section id, splices the section at the requested position, and saves the updated pages.

symbols.does: Reorders a section within a page.
symbols.description: It splices the section out of the source index and into the destination index in a copied sections array. It then selects the moved section at its new index.

symbols.does: Copies a section immediately after itself.
symbols.description: It shallow-copies the section and its data, gives the duplicate a timestamp id, and inserts it after the original.

symbols.does: Toggles a section's locked flag.
symbols.description: It maps to the targeted section and flips its locked value without changing other section data.

symbols.does: Inserts a new page or slide.
symbols.description: It creates a blank page for a default trailing blank slide, otherwise builds a page from WS_buildPage, inserts it at the requested index, selects it, and clears section selection.

symbols.does: Copies a page and inserts it after the source page.
symbols.description: It duplicates the page, assigns new ids to the page and its sections, shallow-copies section data, selects the copy, and shows a slide duplicated toast.

symbols.does: Removes a page after confirmation.
symbols.description: It prevents deleting the last slide, asks for confirmation, filters out the page, and adjusts currentPage to remain in range.

symbols.does: Reorders pages within the doc.
symbols.description: It splices the moved page from its source position into the destination position and then selects that destination page.

symbols.does: Sets the page kind and clears its motif.
symbols.description: It maps to the target page and replaces kind while setting motif to null, leaving other pages unchanged.

symbols.does: Sets the motif for one page.
symbols.description: It maps to the target page and stores the provided motif value.

symbols.does: Sets the document theme.
symbols.description: It saves the theme value on the doc and announces the resolved theme name from WS_THEMES.

symbols.does: Inserts a resolved section at a page position.
symbols.description: It validates the section kind against WS_BLOCKS, merges block defaults with supplied data, inserts a new timestamp-id section, and selects the inserted index.

symbols.does: Applies a layout to the current page.
symbols.description: It ignores blank layouts, builds a page with WS_buildPage, replaces the current page's kind and sections from that built page, and emits an applied-layout toast.

symbols.does: Resolves and applies a library payload.
symbols.description: It uses WS_resolveLibraryPayload, routes layout payloads to applyLayout, and inserts block payloads at the end of the current page.

symbols.does: Handles drag-and-drop library insertion onto the document preview.
symbols.description: It reads application/x-cv-library data, parses JSON, passes it through handleLibraryInsert, and clears the slide drop active state.

symbols.does: Asks Claude to revise one block's data from a user message.
symbols.description: It builds a prompt with doc type, block type, current JSON data, and brand voice constraints; parses compact JSON from the reply; merges parsed values into the section data; and reports success or failure.

symbols.does: Asks Claude for multiple data variations of a block.
symbols.description: It prompts for three same-structure variations, parses a variations array, stores up to five variations keyed by section id, and uses refiningIdx for busy state.

symbols.does: Applies or clears a stored block variation.
symbols.description: When variIdx is null it clears the active variation marker. Otherwise it merges the selected variation data into the section and records the active variation index.

symbols.does: Asks Claude to compose blocks for one page.
symbols.description: It builds context from prior pages, requests two to four supported block kinds as compact JSON, filters to registered WS_BLOCKS, replaces the page's sections with generated sections, and reports the number filled.

symbols.does: Renders built-in and registry-provided document type choices.
symbols.description: It shows active built-in DOC_TYPES with optional TypeThumb previews and inactive items as coming soon. It also renders extra registry doc Types with provenance, layer badge, family, and extends metadata.

symbols.does: Renders the draft setup panel for a new document.
symbols.description: It shows the selected doc type, brief textarea, cancel and start actions, and type-specific pickers for widget kind/system or wizard kind. Placeholder text changes by doc type.

symbols.does: Renders a deck slide preview frame.
symbols.description: The component is used around deck SectionList or LayoutPicker content. Its props include doc, page, motif, bleed, slide index, and slide count, indicating it frames slide content with document theme and pagination context.

symbols.does: Recomputes slide frame fit measurements.
symbols.description: The symbol is a local helper associated with preview fitting. It exists as a standalone function named fit inside the file and is tied to sizing behavior for rendered canvas content.

symbols.does: Renders brochure page preview content.
symbols.description: The component wraps the brochure branch of the editor around either LayoutPicker or SectionList. Its visible props are page and children.

symbols.does: Renders layout choices and AI fill or proposal actions for empty pages.
symbols.description: It is used for empty deck and brochure pages, receives docType, onPick, onAiFill, aiBusy, and onAiPropose, and triggers either layout application or Vi-driven page generation.

symbols.does: Renders and manages editable sections on a page.
symbols.description: It receives page data, selection state, refining state, mutation callbacks, variation state, block insertion callbacks, and Vi insertion callbacks. In the shown call sites it is the editable block list for non-empty pages.

symbols.does: Renders a draggable section item.
symbols.description: The symbol list identifies it as a component accepting props. Its role is tied to SectionList's move and reorder behavior in the same file.

symbols.does: Renders deck page thumbnails and slide controls.
symbols.description: It is used only for deck docs and receives currentPage, pick, add, move, duplicate, remove callbacks, and motif resolver. The call site places it below the slide preview.

symbols.does: Renders the deck outline list.
symbols.description: It appears in the right outline panel and receives doc, currentPage, pick, move, and add callbacks. It complements the SlideStrip with an outline-oriented page navigation surface.

symbols.does: Renders variable reference controls in the right rail.
symbols.description: It is included for both deck and non-deck edit rails. The symbol list also shows local helpers for recounting references, adding variables, auditing, and accepting audit results.

symbols.does: Refreshes variable reference counts from WS_VARS.
symbols.description: It sets refs from window.WS_VARS.countReferences() or a default empty counts object. The function is written as a const arrow assigned inside VariablesRail.

symbols.does: Adds a variable from the VariablesRail UI.
symbols.description: The symbol list identifies it as a local VariablesRail function. Its name and neighboring helpers indicate it creates a new variable entry through the variable rail workflow.

symbols.does: Runs a variable audit from VariablesRail.
symbols.description: It is an async local function associated with variable auditing. Neighboring accept helpers indicate it produces suggestions or findings that can be accepted into variable state.

symbols.does: Accepts one variable audit result.
symbols.description: It receives a value v and applies a single audit recommendation in the VariablesRail workflow. It is paired with acceptAll for batch acceptance.

symbols.does: Accepts all variable audit results.
symbols.description: It applies the accumulated audit recommendations from VariablesRail in one action. The symbol name indicates batch acceptance rather than single-item acceptance.

symbols.does: Renders Vi suggestions for the active doc, page, and selection.
symbols.description: It receives doc, currentPage, selectedIdx, and onRun, and is displayed in the right rail for deck and non-deck editing. The rail is used to trigger candidate-gallery actions.

symbols.does: Dismisses a Vi suggestion by id.
symbols.description: It is a local function inside ViSuggestionsRail. It removes or hides a suggestion from the rail's displayed suggestions state.

symbols.does: Renders the save-as-template extraction preview modal.
symbols.description: It receives preview state, close and confirm handlers, a template name changer, and a variable patch callback. The Workshop passes it after template extraction and also in widget and wizard builder branches.

symbols.does: Renders the document preview inside the template preview flow.
symbols.description: It receives the preview object and is associated with the TemplatePreviewModal. The symbol name indicates a render-only subcomponent for visualizing the extracted template.

symbols.does: Renders a labeled option picker for draft setup.
symbols.description: It receives label, value, onChange, and options and is used by NewDocPanel for widget kind, widget system, and wizard kind selection.

symbols.does: Renders theme, motif, and page-kind controls.
symbols.description: It receives the current theme, theme setter, page motif setter, page kind setter, and close handler. Workshop shows it when showThemeBar is true.

symbols.does: Renders a block-add control.
symbols.description: It receives onAdd and belongs to the section and block insertion surface. The symbol name indicates it opens or presents available block choices.

symbols.does: Generates draft pages for a deck or brochure from a brief.
symbols.description: It is called by startDraft for non-widget and non-wizard AI drafting. Its result is assigned to pages for the new doc.

symbols.does: Returns default pages for a document type.
symbols.description: It is used when starting blank and as a fallback when AI drafting fails. Widget and wizard drafting also call it for the pages portion of their new document.

symbols.does: Returns type-specific extra doc fields for blank creation.
symbols.description: It is used when not AI drafting and as a catch fallback in startDraft. It receives type, kind, and system so widget or wizard metadata can be initialized.

symbols.does: Returns a default title for a doc type.
symbols.description: It is used when a new doc has no title derived from the brief. The title is stored on the new doc before saving.

symbols.does: Derives a document title from the draft brief.
symbols.description: It is used during new doc creation before falling back to titleForType. It converts user-entered brief text into a saved title.

symbols.does: Parses JSON from model replies with tolerant extraction.
symbols.description: It is used by refineSection, generateVariations, and fillSlideWithVi after prompts ask for compact JSON. The helper provides a shared parse path for AI text responses.
